hypothesis: If a hybrid Transformer–Mamba encoder–decoder is pretrained to align seven textual molecular representations (e.g., SMILES, SELFIES, InChI, IUPAC, formula, polymer SPG, electrolytes), then it will learn a representation-invariant latent space that clusters by chemical similarity, achieve higher accuracy on diverse molecular/polymer/electrolyte property prediction than single-representation models, and translate accurately between representations (high validity and structural fidelity) because multimodal alignment forces capture of shared, representation-agnostic chemical semantics.
